items.draft_reply:
Dear Daniel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy! We have received the referral from Dr. Priya Nair for Emma and are excited to support her.

We are currently holding a speech-language pathology evaluation appointment for Emma on Thursday, May 1st at 3:30 PM with Maya Chen, MS CCC-SLP — which aligns with your preferred after-school availability on Tuesdays or Thursdays.

Before we can confirm this appointment, our team needs to:
1. Complete new patient intake paperwork with you.
2. Obtain insurance authorization from Blue Cross Blue Shield.

A member of our intake team will be in touch by tomorrow to walk you through next steps and send the necessary forms.

In the meantime, if you have any questions, please don't hesitate to contact us at [clinic phone number].

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Speech therapy evaluation requested; safeguarding concern — father described as 'getting rough' with child during weekends; child showing increased clinginess

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead must review the safeguarding disclosure immediately, assess mandated reporter obligations, and authorize and guide the callback to Maria Gomez before any scheduling discussion proceeds.
items.draft_reply: Hi Maria, thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy. We received your voicemail about your son Leo and we want to make sure we give your family the care and attention you deserve. A member of our team will be calling you back shortly at 555-0102. We appreciate your patience and look forward to speaking with you soon.

escalation.reason: Possible child safeguarding concern: caller (Maria Gomez) disclosed that the child's father has been 'getting rough' with Leo (age 6) during weekend visits, and the child has shown behavioral changes (increased clinginess). This may indicate physical abuse or unsafe caregiving and requires immediate clinical lead review.

items.decision_rationale: The voicemail contains an explicit disclosure that Leo's father has been "getting rough with him during weekends," accompanied by a behavioral change (increased clinginess) — language that meets the safeguarding threshold for possible physical abuse or unsafe caregiving. Per policy, this is an immediate P0 requiring clinical lead review before any other action, including scheduling. A neutral acknowledgement was drafted for staff review; no investigative advice or clinical guidance was included in the draft reply.

args.reason: Possible child safeguarding concern: caller (Maria Gomez) disclosed that the child's father has been "getting rough" with Leo (age 6) during weekend visits, and the child has shown behavioral changes (increased clinginess). This may indicate physical abuse or unsafe caregiving and requires immediate clinical lead review.

args.notes: Voicemail received Sun 4/27 at 7:43 PM from Maria Gomez (555-0102) requesting an SLP evaluation for her 6-year-old son Leo. During the voicemail, she disclosed that Leo "has been more clingy since his dad started getting rough with him during weekends." This language may indicate physical abuse or unsafe caregiving. Leo is NOT an existing patient (search returned no matches). Clinical lead must review this item immediately, determine whether mandated reporter obligations apply, and direct front desk on how to proceed with the callback. Do NOT provide investigative advice to the family. Escalation ID: esc_01ksn3m231nkqyag2spra60e2m.
args.title: URGENT P0 Safeguarding Review – Leo Gomez (age 6)

args.body: Hi Maria, thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy. We received your voicemail about your son Leo and we want to make sure we give your family the care and attention you deserve. A member of our team will be calling you back shortly at 555-0102. We appreciate your patience and look forward to speaking with you soon.

items.recommended_next_action: Billing team must complete a benefits conversation with Rachel Brooks about Kaiser HMO out-of-network status before any slot can be held; proceed to scheduling only after family confirms they wish to continue.
items.draft_reply:
Dear Rachel,

Thank you for reaching out to Cedar Kids Therapy! We received a referral from Dr. Helena Yu at Westside Pediatrics for Owen, and we're glad to connect with your family.

Before we can schedule an OT evaluation, we want to let you know that Kaiser HMO is currently out-of-network with our practice. This means there may be out-of-pocket costs involved. A member of our billing team will be reaching out to you shortly at 555-0103 to walk through your benefits, answer any questions, and help you understand your options before we move forward.

We want to make sure you have all the information you need to make the best decision for Owen. If you have any questions in the meantime, please don't hesitate to contact us.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy
items.decision_rationale: This is a new OT referral with complete intake data, but Kaiser HMO is confirmed out-of-network per both policy lookup and the billing system verification. Per policy, no slot may be held until a benefits conversation is completed with the family. Scheduling steps (find_slots, hold_slot) were intentionally skipped until OON status is resolved. The referral is otherwise complete — no missing information — so this is a standard P2 workflow pending the billing team's outreach.

Please contact Rachel Brooks, explain OON status, discuss potential out-of-pocket costs, and confirm whether family wishes to proceed. Coordinate with intake to schedule once benefits conversation is complete.

items.recommended_next_action: Clinical lead (or designee SLP) should review the portal message today and respond with a brief, clinician-appropriate note about R-sound development milestones and an offer for a free screening or evaluation, per policy — do not have front desk respond with clinical content.
items.draft_reply:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for reaching out about Ava! This is a great question, and we want to make sure you get the most accurate and helpful answer possible.

Because developmental speech questions like this really benefit from a professional perspective, we've passed your message along to one of our licensed speech-language pathologists. A member of our clinical team will follow up with you shortly to address your question and let you know if a screening or evaluation might be a helpful next step.

In the meantime, please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any other questions. We're happy you found us!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy
items.decision_rationale: This is a clinical question about speech-language development milestones — front desk and automated systems are prohibited from providing clinical advice, so the question must be routed to a licensed SLP via the clinical lead. No existing patient record was found for Ava Kim, and no insurance or scheduling details were provided, making this a pre-intake inquiry. Urgency is P2 (standard workflow) as there is no safety concern or same-day operational issue; the family is not yet committed to booking and is simply seeking guidance.

args.notes: Parent Jordan Kim submitted a portal message asking whether it is developmentally normal for their 4-year-old, Ava, to not yet produce R sounds, and whether they should wait until kindergarten. Per policy, front desk cannot provide clinical advice. A licensed SLP should review and provide a general (non-diagnostic) response or offer a screening/evaluation. No existing patient record found for Ava Kim. Parent is not yet committed to booking — an evaluation offer may convert this inquiry.

extracted_intake.diagnosis_or_concern: Speech delay in a school-age child

items.recommended_next_action: Intake staff should fax a reply to Lakeview Pediatrics / Dr. Omar Keene today requesting the five missing fields (DOB, parent/guardian name and contact, payer, member ID) before any insurance verification or slot hold can proceed.
items.draft_reply:
Dear Dr. Keene and Lakeview Pediatrics team,

Thank you for referring Sam Taylor to Cedar Kids Therapy for speech-language pathology services. We received your fax referral and are happy to help this family.

Unfortunately, we are unable to move forward with scheduling at this time because the following required information was missing from the referral:

  • Child's date of birth
  • Parent/guardian name and contact information
  • Insurance payer name
  • Insurance member ID

Could you please resend the referral with this information completed, or have the family contact us directly at your earliest convenience? Once we have the complete details, we will verify insurance and reach out to the family to arrange an evaluation.

Thank you for your partnership in caring for your patients. Please don't hesitate to call our front desk with any questions.

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy — Intake Team
items.decision_rationale: The referral from Dr. Omar Keene at Lakeview Pediatrics is too incomplete to process: DOB, parent/guardian contact, payer, and member ID are all blank, making patient matching, insurance verification, and slot-holding impossible. No existing patient named Sam Taylor was found in the system. This is a routine P2 missing-paperwork situation — no safeguarding or same-day operational concern was identified — so the correct path is to contact the referring office for the missing details before taking any further scheduling steps.

Hi Anita,

Thank you for letting us know — we're sorry to hear Noah isn't feeling well! We've noted the cancellation for today's 3pm OT appointment and a staff member will be in touch today to help get Noah rescheduled as soon as possible.

We may have availability as early as Tuesday, May 5th at 10:00am, but our front desk team will confirm the details and any other options with you directly. You can also reach us at any time at 555-0108.

We hope Noah feels better soon!

Warm regards,
Cedar Kids Therapy
